label: Gallbladder etc.
quick_take: Gallbladder cancer is a classic "female excess + geographic belt" story. Chile is the global hotspot and stays that way. The unusual finding is the steep *decline* in Israel (both Jews and non-Jews) — a fall that almost nobody has written about.

findings.title: Chile remains the world capital
findings.text: Valdivia women 27.3 (vol IX), 25.1 (X), 18.9 (XI); Bío Bío 24.6 (X). All 10–15× the world median. Reassuringly stable and well documented.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Chile remains the world capital

findings.title: Israel's collapse is the biggest underreported finding here
findings.text: Jewish women went 11.5 (vol II) → 1.4 (vol XI) — 87 % down. The whole-Israel line followed at 11.4 → 1.6. European-origin Israeli-Jewish women peaked at 22.8 in vol III and fell 86 % to 2.0 by vol VIII. This is on the same order as the Chilean excess — but falling.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Israel's collapse is the biggest underreported finding here

findings.title: USA New Mexico American-Indian women: ASR 31.4 (vol IV)
findings.text: Third-highest global outlier, 9.4× median. An underappreciated population-specific hotspot — but the vol IV figure is based on a small Native-subpopulation numerator and predates the recoverable volume window for most cross-registry plots; pop_warning NM vol VIII.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — USA New Mexico American-Indian women: ASR 31.4 (vol IV)

findings.title: Algeria Sétif: severe female-to-male skew
findings.text: Women 3.94 vs men 0.74 (vol VI) → 9.99 vs 2.09 (vol IX), a 5:1 female:male ratio. Global vol-VI median is close to 1:1 the other way. Shared by a handful of North-African / Caribbean registries. Both sexes show ASR rising while crude falling — population ageing is part of the story.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Algeria Sétif: severe female-to-male skew

findings.title: Rising tide in East and South Asia
findings.text: India Chennai men 0.15 → 1.82 (n=1→222); China Qidong women 0.44 → 2.71 (n=12→224); Singapore Chinese women 0.45 → 2.04 (n=14→312). Baselines are very small (≤14 cases in early volumes), percent changes are inflated; absolute final levels are modest but the direction is consistent across the region.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Rising tide in East and South Asia

findings.title: Bimodal age curves absent; peaks sit in the usual 70+ window
findings.text: **Bimodal age curves absent; peaks sit in the usual 70+ window.**
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Bimodal age curves absent; peaks sit in the usual 70+ window

findings.text: ICD reorganisation around gallbladder / extrahepatic bile duct / ampulla has moved codes between sites across ICD-8/9/10. Some of the Israel fall is likely to be real, but some may reflect reallocation of ampullary tumours to C24 → C17 boundaries; worth verifying against registry-level notes.
Chilean registries have had strong secular ascertainment improvements — level is credible, trend should be stated cautiously.

findings.title: Why is gallbladder cancer falling so fast in Israel?
findings.text: Classic risk factors (gallstones, obesity, parity, Mexican-Native/Mapuche genetics) don't obviously explain it. Early cholecystectomy rates plus change in obstetric exposures is one hypothesis.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Why is gallbladder cancer falling so fast in Israel?

findings.text: Pairing Chile's level with Israel's fall would make a striking "high plateau vs disappearing excess" story.
North African female excess merits an investigation into radiographic-contrast history and obstetric factors.
